rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant Caldera Pharmeceuticals, Inc., A Delaware Limited Liability Company'S Demurrer To Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 6, 2009, line 26, Cross-Defendant Caldera Pharmeceuticals, Inc., A Delaware Limited Liability Company's Demurrer To Cross-Complaint. The demurrer of Dr. Woods is overruled; 10 days to answer. The substance of the employment agreement is plead and the demurrer to the tort claims is overruled for the reasons stated in connection with the Caldera demurrer. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant Caldera Pharmeceuticals, Inc., A Delaware Limited Liability Company'S Demurrer To Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 6, 2009, line 26, Cross-Defendant Caldera Pharmeceuticals, Inc., A Delaware Limited Liability Company's Demurrer To Cross-Complaint. The demurrer to LANS' cross-complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part with 10 days leave to amend. It is overruled as to the first cause of action, first count, for LANS need not allege compliance with the notice/default provision for the reasons stated in connection with the demurrer to the Regents' cross-complaint; causation and damages are adequately alleged. The demurrer is sustained as to the first cause of action, second count and third count, which must be plead, as a matter of form, as causes of action, rather than counts, as an alternative theories of liability. Williams v. Marshall (1951) 37 Cal.2d 445, 457. The demurrer is sustained as to the second cause of action for LANS to plead, as distinct causes of action, breach of the option agreement and the license agreement. The demurrer is overruled as to the fourth cause of action for inducing breach of contract. Paragraphs 12-17 plead the requisite terms of the contract. There are factual issues as to what capacity Dr. Warner was acting in at the time of the alleged breach thereby precluding sustaining the demurrer. Woods v. Fox Broadcasting Sub. Inc. (2005) !29 Cal.App.4th 344, 355-356. Damages are adequately alleged. The demurrer to the tort claims is overruled for the reasons stated in connection with the Caldera demurrer. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant Caldera Pharmeceuticals, Inc., A Delaware Limited Liability Company'S Demurrer To Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 6, 2009, line 26, Cross-Defendant Caldera Pharmeceuticals, Inc., A Delaware Limited Liability Company's Demurrer To Cross-Complaint. Caldera's demurrer to the Regents' cross-complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part with 10 days leave to amend. It is sustained as to the first cause of action for the Regents have alleged a breach of two contracts and must plead the breach of each contract in a separate cause of action. The Regents need not allege compliance with the notice/default provision of the license agreement for it is, at most, a condition of termination of the contract, not a condition to a suit for breach. Moreover, the language is permissive. Causation and damages are adequately alleged. The demurrer is overruled as to the second cause of action based on Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97 and the third cause of action based on Thrifty-Tel, Inc. v. Bezenek (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1559. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JILL TOUPS VS. CHRISTOPHER RANSOM JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 6, 2009, line 15, PLAINTIFF JILL TOUPS Motion For Preliminary Injunction. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS DEMONSTRATED IRREPARABLE HARM. THE FIDELITY ESCROW REPORT AND SPATARO'S DECLARATION RAISE SERIOUS QUESTIONS AS TO WHETHER SPATARO MADE THE LOAN WITHOUT KNOWLEDGE OR A REASON TO BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF CLAIMED AN INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO RUSSELL DECLARATION PARAGRAPH 1 - SUSTAINED, PARAGRAPH 2 - OVERRULED, PARAGRAPH 3 - SUSTAINED AS TO 2ND SENTENCE, PARAGRAPH 4 - OVERRULED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 6, 2009, line 18, RESPONDENT BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED FOR PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE A BENEFICIAL INTEREST. PLAINTIFF'S INTEREST CONSISTED OF HAVING A PERMIT TO REMOVE THE SIGNS; ONCE REMOVED PLAINTIFF HAD NO FURTHER INTEREST. COMPETITION WITH THE LEE'S IS NOT A SUFFICIENT INTEREST. PLAINTIFF REQUESTS LEAVE TO AMEND BUT FAILS TO SPECIFY WHAT FACTS PLAINTIFF COULD ALLEGE TO DEMONSTRATE STANDING. =(302/PJM/BD)
